strategy: Use Round 1 freely; the cheats do not begin until Round 2. Set up blades and a trap early while it is safe.

Track the Beast's Polymorph. Each shift casts a specific aura (Ice/Sleet Storm, Life/Devotion, Fire/Furnace, Myth/Reliquary, Storm/Galvanic Field, Death/Virulence) that dictates which two schools may safely damage it that turn.
Match your hit to the safe schools for the active aura. Hitting with a wrong school triggers a big retaliation spell (Lord of Winter, Spinysaur, Sun Serpent, and so on).
Never damage it while its kill cheat is armed. Killed with the cheat active, it revives to 100%. Strip or wait out the aura, or land your finisher only with the currently safe school.
Do damage every round. At the end of any round it took no damage, it free-casts a random AoE (Blizzard, Tempest, Meteor Strike, etc.), so keep chipping even between big setups.
It is Moon with 59% Storm resist but takes 30% boosted Myth, so a Myth hitter fits many of the safe-school windows; watch multi-school spells since each school is checked separately.
cheatNotes: Its cheats are dormant on Round 1, giving you one free setup turn before you must play around the aura rules.
Right after it Polymorphs, a cheat fires based on the shift it took, applying the aura that sets that turn's safe damage schools.
If you kill it while the retaliation cheat is active it revives to full Health, so clear or account for the aura before the finisher.
Under Sleet Storm, only Fire and Myth are safe; anything else triggers Lord of Winter.
Under Devotion, only Death and Storm are safe; anything else triggers Spinysaur.
Under Furnace, only Life and Storm are safe; anything else triggers Sun Serpent.
Under Reliquary, only Fire and Life are safe; anything else triggers Celestial Calendar.
Under Galvanic Field, only Death and Ice are safe; anything else triggers Storm Owl.
Under Virulence, only Ice and Myth are safe; anything else triggers Avenging Fossil.
Multi-school spells like Hydra check each school separately; if any one school is unsafe, the retaliation fires once.
End any round without dealing damage to it and it free-casts one random AoE from its list, so always land at least a chip hit.
